content.problemStatement:
Today a plane was hijacked by a maniac. All the passengers of the flight are taken as hostage. Chef is also one of them.

He invited one of the passengers to play a game with him. If he loses the game, he will release all the passengers, otherwise he will kill all of them. A high risk affair it is.



Chef volunteered for this tough task. He was blindfolded by Hijacker. Hijacker brought a big black bag from his pockets. The contents of the bag is not visible. He tells Chef that the bag contains **R** red, **G** green and **B** blue colored balloons.



Hijacker now asked Chef to take out some balloons from the box such that there are at least **K** balloons of the same color and hand him over. If the taken out balloons does not contain at least **K** balloons of the same color, then the hijacker will shoot everybody. Chef is very scared and wants to leave this game as soon as possible, so he will draw the minimum number of balloons so as to save the passengers. Can you please help scared Chef to find out the minimum number of balloons he should take out.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line has three space‑separated integers R, G and B – the numbers of red, green and blue balloons in the bag. The second line contains a single integer K.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with one integer – the minimum number of balloons Chef must draw to be certain that among the drawn balloons there are at least K balloons of the same colour.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For K=1, Chef needs only one draw. For K=2, the worst case is drawing one balloon of each colour (3 draws); the fourth draw guarantees a colour appears twice.
limit = K-1 = 2. Chef can draw at most min(2,2)+min(5,2)+min(4,2)=2+2+2=6 balloons without having three of the same colour. The next draw forces three of some colour, so answer is 7.
limit = 4. Safe draws = min(10,4)+min(1,4)+min(1,4)=4+1+1=6. One more draw gives at least 5 red balloons.

solution.approach: For a fixed K, Chef wants the worst‑case (maximum) number of draws that can still avoid having K balloons of any colour. From each colour he can draw at most K‑1 balloons without reaching K; if a colour has fewer than K‑1 balloons, he can draw all of them. Therefore the maximum safe draws are sum_{c∈{R,G,B}} min(c, K‑1). The next draw (i.e. +1) inevitably forces some colour to reach K because at least one colour still has a balloon left (K ≤ max(R,G,B)). Hence the answer is sum_{c} min(c, K‑1) + 1. The computation is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each test case.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        R = int(next(it)); G = int(next(it)); B = int(next(it))
        K = int(next(it))
        limit = K - 1
        total = min(R, limit) + min(G, limit) + min(B, limit)
        out.append(str(total + 1))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
